title: Harvard grad student workers end 40-day strike without a contract
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Harvard graduate student workers ended a 40-day strike without a new contract, following a shift in university negotiations.
The union representing over 4,000 students will resume negotiations on June 9 and June 23, focusing on key issues.
Harvard has proposed a 2.75% raise upon contract ratification, with an additional 3.25% increase planned for July 1.

title: A.J. Brown trade grades: Did experts think Patriots gave up too much for star WR?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots traded a 2028 first-round pick and a 2027 fifth-round pick for A.J. Brown.
Experts gave the Patriots favorable grades, seeing Brown as a valuable addition to improve their offense.
The Philadelphia Eagles received mixed reviews, praised for obtaining draft picks despite Brown's departure.

title: 7News anchor leaving the station after 6 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Amanda Crawford is leaving WHDH-TV's 7News after nearly six years, announcing her departure in a social media post.
Crawford has enjoyed a 17-year journalism career, working across the U.S. in South Carolina, Florida, Nebraska, and Virginia.
She plans to leave TV news for a field offering more flexibility, without disclosing specific future career plans.

title: 3 ways A.J. Brown will likely impact Patriots offense, Drake Maye in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots acquire A.J. Brown from Eagles in exchange for 2028 first-round and 2027 fifth-round picks.
A.J. Brown recorded 22 receiving touchdowns against man coverage since joining the NFL in 2022.
Brown's arrival might lead to trade considerations for Kayshon Boutte within the Patriots' receiver corps.

title: Norton bakery apologizes for customer-requested Hitler cake posted on Facebook
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sweet Stuff Bake Shop in Norton apologized for a Hitler-themed cake featuring Cartman from 'South Park,' requested by a customer.
The bakery's Facebook post of the cake sparked backlash, leading to its removal and a public apology.
Reactions to the apology were mixed, with some defending the bakery and others upset about the cake's Nazi imagery.

title: How windshields can affect tinted eyeglasses
author: John Paul
bullets: Photochromic lenses darken in sunlight due to UV exposure, but most windshields block UV light, preventing tint activation.
Modern vehicle windshields filter UV light besides providing structural integrity and airbag support, affecting photochromic lenses.
Manual sunglasses are recommended while driving since no modern cars have windshields that allow necessary UV for lens activation.

title: Mass. police department cancels appearance of Trump-backed ‘Freedom Truck’ over ties to right-wing organization
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Westfield Police Department canceled the 'Freedom Truck' event due to its political affiliations harming public trust.
The 'Freedom Truck', linked to PragerU and funded by federal agencies, was set to visit Westfield State University.
Freedom 250 events have seen cancellations, including Martina McBride and other artists withdrawing from the 'Great American State Fair.'

title: DA: Man charged in Pittsfield fatal stabbing had ‘no prior connection’ to victim
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Zyrus Jaynes, 24, charged with murder for allegedly fatally stabbing Jesse Gray, 36, in Pittsfield on May 29.
Police detained Jaynes in Pittsfield the following day; he pled not guilty and is held without bail.
District Attorney confirmed no prior connection between Jaynes and Gray before the fatal altercation.

title: Patriots lose new tight end for 2026 season due to injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Julian Hill will miss the 2026 NFL season due to being placed on injured reserve by the New England Patriots.
Hill signed a three-year, $15 million deal with the Patriots in March to serve as backup to Hunter Henry.
2026 third-round pick Eli Raridon may take on a larger role in Hill's absence, with further roster changes possible.

title: 5 clues A.J. Brown dropped about his desire to join the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown had tears after Patriots passed on him in 2019 NFL Draft, choosing N'Keal Harry instead.
In 2024, Brown stirred speculation by changing his Instagram profile to a photo of Tom Brady in a Patriots jersey.
During an interview with Julian Edelman, Brown hinted interest in Patriots by winking when asked about joining the team.
